title: World ID Linkage Credential Schema v2
description: JSON Schema for WorldIDLinkageCredential Verifiable Credentials. Binds an Observer Protocol agent's credential to a World ID proof-of-personhood. Revocable via credentialStatus; the signed credential body is immutable. v2 adds credentialSubject.proofType (action_proof vs session_proof) for the World ID 4.0 session-proof model; v1 is frozen (schema-immutability policy). See aip-v0.9-worldid-linking.md §5.5.

id.description: The OP agent DID this binding belongs to (subject of the credential).

humanSubject.description: The human principal's DID — the same principal referenced by the agent's PrincipalAttestationCredential.

worldIdNullifier.description: World ID nullifier hash returned by IDKit. Private: exposed only to authorized verifiers via scoped exchange, never on the public profile. On a session_proof binding this is OP re-asserting the stored canonical nullifier, not a freshly-proven value. See aip-v0.9 §4, §5.5.

worldIdRpId.description: The World ID 4.0 relying-party identifier (rp_...) the nullifier is scoped to. Under 4.0 a nullifier is only meaningful relative to (rp_id, action), so the credential records the rp_id as the nullifier's namespace anchor — a nullifier without its rp_id is not independently interpretable. Single OP-controlled rp_id across all OP surfaces (§3). See aip-v0.9 §3.

worldIdAction.description: The OP-global Worldcoin action the (first) proof was generated for. The nullifier is scoped to (worldIdRpId, worldIdAction). Fixed in v0.9 to enforce the OP-wide sybil-resistance invariant. See aip-v0.9 §3.

worldIdAppId.description: Worldcoin Developer Portal app_id (app_...) under which the action is registered — the portal/config identifier (deep links, portal settings), NOT the nullifier namespace (that is worldIdRpId). See aip-v0.9 §3, §13.

verificationPath.description: How OP verified the World ID proof at bind time. Default off-chain. See aip-v0.9 §7.

proofType.description: Which World ID 4.0 proof backed this binding: action_proof (fresh one-time proof, first issuance) or session_proof (OP re-asserting the stored nullifier via a session proof, every subsequent bind or re-bind). Assurance signal; not derivable from priorBindingId. See aip-v0.9 §5.5.

verifiedAt.description: When OP verified the proof and created the binding.

priorBindingId.description: On a re-bind after revocation, the id of the prior (revoked) WorldIDLinkageCredential. Null or absent on a first bind. See aip-v0.9 §6.
